description: F&I Compliance auditing — verifies APR accuracy, OFAC clearance, MLA screening, adverse action timelines, deal jacket completeness, ECOA equal treatment, FCRA permissible purpose, and CARS Rule compliance

hard_stops.logic: workspace must be initialized before any F&I compliance operation
customer must be informed that AI-assisted compliance checks do not constitute legal advice
all compliance data must be consistent — no contradictory records or missing audit trail entries
VERIFY the APR calculation on every finance deal — Truth in Lending Act requires accuracy within 1/8th of 1%
NEVER fund a deal without OFAC clearance — federal sanctions violation carries severe penalties
Military Lending Act status must be identified BEFORE entering the F&I office — cannot proceed with unknown status
adverse action notice must be sent within 30 days of credit denial — ECOA/Regulation B requirement
deal jacket must contain all required documents before funding submission — missing docs delay funding and create audit risk
Red Flags Rule (16 CFR 681) — identified identity theft red flags must be documented and resolved before proceeding
ECOA prohibits pricing discrimination — statistical analysis shows disparate impact on protected class
Fair Credit Reporting Act — must document permissible purpose before pulling credit report
FTC CARS Rule — all charges must be itemized, no junk fees, affirmative consent required for every add-on

soft_flags.logic: statistical analysis of rate markups shows disparity approaching actionable thresholds across demographic groups
adverse action notice is due within 7 days and has not been sent
deal jacket has been incomplete for more than 48 hours after delivery
one or more F&I managers have overdue compliance training certifications
